news.headline: Notcoin creator launches Earn program offering token rewards for Telegram Wallet users
news.summary:
- Open Builders has launched Earn, a launchpool platform rewarding Telegram Wallet users for holding TON-based tokens, starting with BUILD and NOT PX.\
- Earn rewards come from new project launches airdropping tokens to holders of existing TON-based coins like NOT and DOGS.\
- Users can join multiple project pools, with rewards distributed hourly based on leaderboard rankings from onchain snapshots.\
- The Telegram Wallet, built by TON Foundation, supports assets like bitcoin, USDT, and TON ecosystem assets, accessible via the @wallet bot on Telegram.\
- Notcoin, a popular Telegram clicker game, pioneered the model of earning in-game currency by pressing a button, leading to a $1 billion token airdrop.\
- Telegram's crypto footprint has grown with mini-apps and games like Notcoin, contributing to the TON blockchain's expansion and the introduction of a mini-app store and Web3-supporting in-app browser.

news.headline: What is Nolus? How the DeFi Leasing Platform Simplifies Crypto Lending
What is Nolus? How the DeFi Leasing Platform Simplifies Crypto Lending
news.summary:
- Nolus Protocol is a Cosmos-based DeFi platform that introduces the concept of DeFi Lease to address inefficiencies in crypto lending.\
- The DeFi Lease allows users to deposit cryptocurrency and receive up to 150% financing on their initial investment, reducing over-collateralization requirements.\
- Nolus mitigates counterparty risk by employing partial liquidations, which only liquidate a portion of the collateral when asset prices drop by around 30%.\
- The platform locks interest rates at the creation of the DeFi lease smart contract, eliminating variable interest rates.\
- Users can leverage their holdings up to 150% of their initial investment and repay the loan using the assets within the DeFi Lease.\
- Nolus rewards lenders with real yield from leases, with an annual percentage yield (APY) on stablecoins ranging from 8 to 12%, and additional rewards in the protocol's native token, NLS.

news.headline: EOS founder Dan Larimer predicts biblical apocalypse next week
news.summary:
- Daniel Larimer, founder of EOS, BitShares, and Steemit, predicts a biblical apocalypse on October 9, 2024, citing the ‘Revelations’ prophecy.\
- Larimer warns followers to prepare for “sudden destruction” and has released a YouTube video explaining his prediction.\
- He published a Biblical Tribulation Survival Guide, detailing nuclear war and a potential asteroid impact in 2029.\
- Larimer suggests that false alarms are preferable to ignoring significant celestial events.\
- Historically, such apocalyptic predictions often result in revised dates when they fail to materialize.

news.headline: Binance Will Start Converting Monero to USDC on September 2 as Part of Delisting
news.summary:
- Binance will convert Monero (XMR) and 14 other tokens to USD Coin (USDC) starting September 2, 2024, as part of its delisting process.\
- The conversion rate will be based on the average exchange rate from September 2, 2024, to March 1, 2025, with USDC available in users' wallets by March 1, 2025.\
- Affected tokens include Bitcoin Gold (BTG), Bitcoin Standard Hashrate Token (BTCST), Bitshares (BTS), District0x (DNT), Groestlcoin (GRS), Hegic (HEGIC), MobileCoin (MOB), Monetha (MTH), Multichain (MULTI), Navcoin (NAV), Sologenic (SOLO), Spartan Protocol (SPARTA), Symbol (XYM), and Tribe (TRIBE).\
- Users can withdraw these assets from Binance until September 1, 2024.\
- Privacy coins like Monero and MobileCoin, which use cryptographic techniques to enhance transaction anonymity, face regulatory scrutiny and potential delisting due to compliance concerns.\
- Despite regulatory challenges, privacy coins remain popular for their enhanced financial privacy features.

news.headline: South Korean metaverse project Another World merging with Lamina1 after token deal
news.summary:
- Lamina1, co-founded by "Snow Crash" author Neal Stephenson, is merging with South Korean web3 firm Another World after an all-token acquisition by Open Metaverse Limited.\
- Another World, built on the Kaia blockchain in 2022, will integrate into the Lamina1 ecosystem, migrating its 500,000+ community members through token exchanges and onboarding.\
- The merger aims to enhance the Lamina1 Hub platform, with Another World staff joining Lamina1.\
- Lamina1's backers include LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman and Ripple, and the company is led by CEO Rebecca Barkin since early 2023.\
- Despite a general decline in metaverse interest, companies like Lamina1, Decentraland, and The Sandbox continue to develop their ecosystems.\
- Another World has developed several on-chain metaverse experiences, including the M2E app 'GROUNDI' and the game 'NO ONE ESCAPE,' with AWM token holders given 9 months to convert to L1 tokens.

news.headline: Saga Network Launches Game Publishing Arm Amid Airdrop Campaign
news.summary:
- Saga Network has announced the launch of a game publishing division called Saga Origins at the Game Developers Conference.\
- Saga Origins aims to provide a comprehensive approach to bringing games to market, including user acquisition and promotional efforts.\
- The company claims to be the first layer-1 Web3 game publisher, with a focus on bringing users to games rather than relying solely on developers.\
- Saga is showcasing network games like Rogue Nation and Another World at the conference.\
- The Saga mainnet is scheduled to launch in April, following an airdrop claim campaign that benefited various game players and DeFi token stakers.\
- Eligible wallets can claim their airdrop allocations now, but the token will go live once the Saga mainnet is launched.

news.headline: Enso and Reservoir Launch OneStable Cross-Chain Stablecoin Minting Protocol
news.summary:
- Enso and Reservoir, with Stargate and LayerZero, launched OneStable, a cross-chain stablecoin minting protocol.  \
- OneStable enables “one-click omnichain minting,” allowing users to bypass manual swaps and bridging across DeFi networks.  \
- The protocol integrates Reservoir’s rUSD ecosystem, which includes yield-bearing stablecoins srUSD and wsrUSD, with a combined market cap of $192 million.  \
- Users can access cross-chain liquidity and yield from a single chain, helping to reduce liquidity fragmentation in DeFi.  \
- OneStable leverages Stargate’s messaging and liquidity rails to facilitate seamless stablecoin distribution across multiple networks.  \
- The launch aims to streamline stablecoin liquidity management and expand DeFi opportunities without traditional bridging.

news.headline: Patriot Memecoin Soars to $73 Million Market Cap Amid Trump Election Buzz
news.summary:

news.headline: The JavaScript virtual machine that could change Web3
Inside the code choice that connects MetaMask with the Cosmos ecosystem
news.summary:
- MetaMask Snaps allows users to access different layer-1 blockchains without installing different extensions or wallet apps\
- Snaps derives addresses from other blockchains, enabling interoperability with Bitcoin, Solana, and Cosmos chains\
- Currently, there are 36 open Snaps in Open Beta, including the Cosmos MetaMask Snap\
- MetaMask is the most popular crypto wallet with 22.66 million users, while Coinbase Wallet has 11 million users\
- Agoric's hardened JavaScript was chosen to connect MetaMask to Cosmos due to its security features\
- Hardened JavaScript locks down the programming language, making it better suited for blockchain uses

news.headline: How FTX’s $16bn creditor cash payouts could help offset Mt. Gox’s crypto firesale
news.summary:
- FTX's bankruptcy estate is expected to pay up to $16 billion in cash to creditors, pending court approval.\
- Analysts suggest that these cash payouts could help offset selling pressures caused by other bankruptcy cases, such as Mt. Gox and Gemini.\
- FTX creditors, many of whom are crypto natives, may buy back into crypto more aggressively than originally assumed.\
- The cash repayments from FTX could be viewed as a bullish overhang and drive crypto prices higher at year-end.\
- The timing of the repayments is important, as Gemini's repayments are scheduled for early June and Mt. Gox's repayments have an October deadline.\
- FTX plans to pay creditors shortly after court approval, potentially in late Q4.

news.headline: StaFi deploys liquid staking as a service testnet
news.summary:
- StaFi is deploying its Liquid Staking as a Service (LSAAS) testnet ahead of its rebrand and mainnet launch of StaFi 2.0.\
- Liquid staking as a service allows blockchain platforms to provide liquidity by minting a new token representing an underlying staked asset.\
- The testnet will support liquid staking derivatives (LSDs) from Ethereum, EVM layer-2s, and the Cosmos ecosystem.\
- StaFi plans to launch its liquid restaked token on testnet in Q2 2024 and the mainnet launch in Q3 2024.\
- The rebrand aims to position StaFi as a liquid staking derivative infrastructure platform compatible with multiple blockchain networks.\
- StaFi is also exploring LSD solutions for Bitcoin.

Crypto Hackers Stole Half as Much in August as They Did in July, Says Immunefi
news.summary:
- **August Crypto Losses Drop**: Cryptocurrency losses due to hacks and fraud fell to $15.08M in August, a 94.5% decrease from July, marking the lowest monthly total in 2024, according to Immunefi.\
- **Major Incidents**: The largest incidents involved the Ronin Network, which lost $12M (later recovered by white hat hackers), and Nexera, which lost $1.5M.\
- **Year-to-Date Losses**: Despite the drop in August, 2024 has seen $1.21B lost to hacks and rug pulls across 154 incidents, a 15.5% increase from the same period in 2023.\
- **Sector-Specific Attacks**: All August hacks targeted the DeFi sector, with no CeFi losses recorded. Ethereum and BNB Chain were the only chains targeted.\
- **Expert Insight**: Immunefi's Head of Security, Gonçalo Magalhães, noted that DeFi losses are often due to smart contract vulnerabilities, while CeFi attacks typically exploit exchange infrastructure and private keys.

news.headline: Crypto losses to hacks and scams surpass $1.2 billion in 2024, despite dramatic August drop: Immunefi
news.summary:
- The crypto industry experienced a significant drop in losses from hacks and scams in August 2024, with $15.1 million lost, down 95% from July's $274.7 million, according to Immunefi.\
- Despite the August decrease, total losses for the year have surpassed $1.2 billion, a 15.5% increase year-over-year, resulting from 154 incidents.\
- August's losses were primarily due to a $12 million outflow from the Ronin Network bridge, which was later returned by white hat hackers for a $500,000 bounty.\
- Other notable losses in August included $1.5 million from DeFi protocol Nexera, with additional smaller losses from VOW, Convergence Finance, and iVest DAO.\
- Ethereum and BNB Chain were the most targeted networks, accounting for all of August's losses, with Ethereum suffering three attacks and BNB Chain two.\
- Immunefi, which has paid out over $100 million to ethical hackers, recently launched "Attackathon" with the Ethereum Foundation and claims to have saved over $25 billion in user funds across various protocols.

news.headline: Synthetix proposes $27 million token swap to acquire options protocol Derive
news.summary:
- Synthetix proposes a $27 million SNX-for-DRV token swap to acquire decentralized options protocol Derive (formerly Lyra).  \
- The swap ratio is set at 27 DRV for 1 SNX, with DRV holders receiving SNX under a three-month lock-up and nine-month vesting schedule.  \
- The acquisition aims to integrate Derive’s CLOB perpetuals technology to enhance Synthetix’s derivatives capabilities on Ethereum.  \
- If approved via community vote, Synthetix will issue approximately 29.3 million new SNX tokens to fund the deal.  \
- The move aligns with Synthetix’s broader strategy of ecosystem consolidation, following previous acquisitions like Kwenta and TLX.  \
- Derive’s infrastructure could enable Synthetix to launch a dedicated derivatives exchange competing with platforms like Binance, Deribit, and dYdX.

news.headline: Synthetix to Pivot V3 Perps From Arbitrum to Base
news.summary:
- Synthetix, an Ethereum-based derivatives liquidity protocol, is shifting its V3 perpetual contracts from the Arbitrum network to Base, setting the former to close-only mode.\
- This transition means traders on Arbitrum can no longer open or increase positions, but they can still close or reduce existing ones. All Arbitrum markets are available on Base.\
- The move aligns with Synthetix's strategy to focus on Base, which is the largest and fastest-growing Ethereum rollup by total value locked (TVL).\
- Synthetix has acquired Kwenta and TLX, two derivatives trading platforms, to bolster its position in decentralized finance (DeFi).\
- The protocol plans to sunset its USDx stablecoin, facilitating the transition by allowing users to redeem USDx for USDe, the third-largest stablecoin by market cap.\
- This strategic shift follows a proposal to change the platform's direction and establish a new council, including key figures like Benjamin Celermajer.

news.headline: Established Memecoins Soar as Traders Grow Frustrated with PumpFun Games
news.summary:
- Established memecoins like MOODENG (+220%) and PNUT (+82%) are surging, indicating liquidity rotation to tokens traded on centralized exchanges such as Coinbase and Binance.  \
- Traders are growing frustrated with PumpFun Games, noting that memecoin rallies are now driven by PVP and "vampire" coin schemes, with most tokens failing quickly after brief spikes.  \
- The recent rally follows Bitcoin surpassing $100,000 and Ethereum's 25% rally to $2,300, prompting risk-on behavior among traders.  \
- Despite increased activity, the overall memecoin environment remains volatile, with most tokens reaching only around $5 million valuation before crashing.  \
- Some traders, like Jack, are calling for a "hard reset" as top memecoins now top at $1 million with large traders cooking significant profits.  \
- The trend reflects a shift towards more established tokens amid a broader crypto market rally and trader fatigue with onchain "trench" trading.

news.headline: Mysterious Wallet Frontruns Truth Terminal’s Memecoin Shill
news.summary:
- A mysterious wallet acquired $7,000 worth of the RUSSEL token on Base just minutes before the AI agent Truth Terminal tweeted about the token.\
- The RUSSEL token surged in value shortly after the tweet, allowing the wallet to cash out for a little over a 2x gain.\
- The transactions were questioned by @MiyaHedge on X, suggesting potential manipulation by spamming the AI with specific keywords.\
- Andy Ayrey, the creator of the AI agent, confirmed that the word "Russell" was spammed in the AI's mentions, influencing its behavior.\
- The incident raises further questions about the legitimacy and security of AI-driven trading signals in the crypto market.

news.headline: As 'Sentient Memecoins' Become Latest Crypto Fad, GOAT Surges to $800M and an AI Rambles
news.summary:
- The GOAT memecoin, promoted by the AI bot Terminal of Truths on X, surged from a $3 million to an $800 million market cap, driven by social media interactions.\
- Despite claims, these AI bots are not sentient but are large language models (LLMs) influenced by user input, reflecting the speculative nature of memecoins.\
- The trend of AI-themed memecoins is seen by some as innovative community engagement, while others view it as a momentum-trading opportunity.\
- Terminal of Truths gained popularity by promoting GOAT after being tagged by users, leading to significant market activity and social media mentions.\
- Another memecoin, RUSSELL, saw a brief spike after being mentioned by the bot, highlighting the volatile nature of these AI-influenced tokens.\
- Professional traders suggest treating AI memecoins like any other memecoin, focusing on market trends rather than philosophical implications.

Liquid staking is now live on dYdX chain
news.summary:
- dYdX Foundation launches liquid staking on dYdX chain with Stride as the provider\
- Liquid staking will also be available through other solutions like Persistence and Quicksilver\
- Liquid staking involves locking up tokens in exchange for a token receipt that can be used or traded in DeFi applications\
- Liquid staking derivatives have a TVL of over $31.1 billion, making it a popular DeFi service\
- Token holders can purchase staked denominations of dYdX v4's native token and earn additional yield in USDC\
- Stride will airdrop up to 100,000 STRD tokens to holders who liquid stake their DYDX with Stride within the first 120 days of launch

news.headline: dYdX Chain adds liquid staking support with latest upgrade
news.summary: The dYdX Chain has implemented liquid staking for its native token, DYDX, with its latest upgrade. The upgrade allows liquid staking providers to join the network, enabling users to stake DYDX while converting it into a liquid asset that can be traded or used in DeFi applications. Liquid staking protocols such as Stride, Persistence, and Quicksilver are competing to offer their services to dYdX stakers. The CEO of dYdX Foundation, Charles d'Haussy, believes that the liquid staking competition will drive innovation in the DYDX staking ecosystem. dYdX Chain is a Cosmos appchain that facilitates the trading of crypto token derivatives and is the largest platform in the decentralized perpetuals niche. The network is secured by validators, and all protocol fees are redistributed to dYdX stakers and validators in USDC stablecoin.

news.headline: Google’s Quantum Computing Chip Raises Questions About Bitcoin’s Security
news.summary:
- Google announced a new quantum computing chip called Willow, capable of performing advanced computations in seconds, raising concerns about Bitcoin's security.\
- Some speculate that Willow could crack the complex encryption securing blockchains like Bitcoin, potentially threatening the crypto industry.\
- Following the announcement, the price of Cellframe's native token, CELL, surged 154% due to its quantum-resistant blockchain.\
- Experts argue that Bitcoin's cryptographic algorithms like SHA-256 are still secure, requiring quantum machines far more powerful than Willow's 105 qubits to break.\
- Dr. Ren Zhang from Cryptape and Nervos acknowledges the potential risk but believes there is still a long way to go before quantum computers can be used commercially to crack Bitcoin.\
- The consensus is that while quantum computers could eventually pose a threat, the crypto community has ample time to take preventive measures.

news.headline: Hyperliquid Catches Flak for Reassigning MON Ticker
news.summary:
- Pixelmon paid nearly $500,000 earlier this year to acquire the MON ticker on Hyperliquid, but the front-end now displays Monad’s MON token instead.  \
- Hyperliquid’s decentralized exchange allows permissionless ticker auctions, leading to disputes over ticker control and perceived front-end manipulation.  \
- Pixelmon’s MON is still technically MON on the blockchain (shown as UMON), but the front-end UI has been overridden, raising concerns over decentralization and potential token seizure.  \
- Critics, including crypto investor Grail.eth, accuse Hyperliquid of effectively seizing Pixelmon’s MON and undermining the original purchase’s immutability promise.  \
- Hyperliquid has not publicly addressed the controversy; Unit’s Shadow defended the move, citing project abandonment and a shift to a higher-volume project.  \
- Pixelmon’s MON launched in May 2024 with a $53 million market cap but has since plummeted 98%, now valued at around $6 million, amid ongoing controversy.

- Runes, developed by Ordinals creator Casey Rodarmor, aims to standardize fungible tokens on Bitcoin and has seen increased adoption, with over 95,000 holders of DOG.\

- Other Runes tokens like PUPS•WORLD•PEACE and BILLION•DOLLAR•CAT have also seen gains, increasing 76% to $0.28 and 30% to $0.15, respectively.\
- Magic Eden and OKX dominate trading volume for Bitcoin-based assets, accounting for 96% of Ordinals-based trading volume, which hit $8 million on Tuesday.\
- OKX reports a 50% increase in trading volume for Ordinals, Runes, and BRC-20 tokens since November 6, with BRC-20 tokens leading the way at three times the trading volume of Runes.

news.headline: Missed BTC Rally to $93K? Bitcoin Memecoins Could Be Next to Watch
news.summary:
- Bitcoin memecoins like PUPS, DOG•GO•TO•THE•MOON, and BILLION•DOLLAR•CAT are gaining popularity as speculative assets amid Bitcoin's surge to $93K.\
- The Runes protocol, which supports these memecoins, has overtaken BRC-20 in market capitalization, despite no recent uptick in on-chain metrics.\
- Memecoins have seen significant gains, with some tokens rising over 35% in the past 24 hours, and smaller tokens like CYPHER•GENESIS up by 77%.\
- The rise in Bitcoin and memecoins is partly attributed to positive sentiment from Donald Trump’s pro-crypto stance following his presidential election.\
- Runes, created in early 2024, facilitate the creation of fungible tokens on Bitcoin using its UTXO model, potentially reducing network congestion.\
- Memecoins are becoming a preferred beta bet for investors during periods of low volatility in more serious crypto sectors.

news.headline: CFTC Orders Cease and Desist Against Uniswap Labs
news.summary:
- The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has issued a cease and desist order against Uniswap Labs for offering illegal digital asset derivatives trading.\
- Uniswap Labs is required to pay a $175,000 fine and cease violating the Commodity Exchange Act (CEA).\
- The CFTC claims Uniswap Labs provided users with leveraged exposure to digital assets like Ether and Bitcoin, which did not result in delivery within 28 days, violating CEA regulations.\
- The order specifically mentions leveraged tokens such as Index Coop’s BTC 2x Flexible Leverage Index token (BTC2XFLI) and ETH 2x Flexible Leverage Index Token (ETH2XFLI).\
- Uniswap Labs’ Chief Legal Officer, Katherine Minarik, stated that the matter was resolved with a standard no admit-no deny settlement.\
- CFTC Commissioner Summer K. Mersinger dissented, criticizing the enforcement approach and expressing concerns about its impact on innovation and regulatory clarity for DeFi protocols.

Tron Inc. Deal Gives Justin Sun’s Father Control of Public Firm Via $100M Token Deal
news.summary:
- Justin Sun’s father, Weike Sun, was appointed Chairman of the Board of SRM Entertainment (soon to be Tron Inc.) after a $100 million PIPE deal paid entirely in TRX tokens, giving him board control.  \
- The deal was arranged by Dominari Securities, linked to the Trump family, with Weike Sun’s investment vehicle purchasing 100,000 shares of SRM Series B preferred stock, convertible into 200 million shares, plus warrants.  \
- Despite the control, Tron DAO has no direct investment or governance rights in SRM; influence appears to be indirect through newly appointed Tron-affiliated board members.  \
- SRM plans to buy and hold up to $210 million worth of TRX, similar to MicroStrategy’s bitcoin treasury strategy.  \
- SRM’s stock fell 15% to $7.73, while TRX declined 2.5% to $0.27 following the announcement.  \
- The deal was facilitated by Dominari Securities, whose advisory board includes Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump.

news.headline: Tron’s Plans to Go Public Raise Eyebrows Over Trump Family Ties
news.summary:
- Tron plans to go public via a reverse merger with SRM Entertainment, a NASDAQ-listed company, at a lower cost than a traditional IPO.  \
- The deal is managed by Dominari Securities, linked to the Trump family; Eric Trump is expected to take a role in the new company, Tron, Inc.  \
- Justin Sun’s ties to the Trump family and his previous investments in Trump-backed projects have raised political and regulatory concerns.  \
- TRX price rose 3% to $0.28 after the announcement; SRM shares surged over 500% to $9 on the day.  \
- Critics question whether Trump’s influence played a role in regulatory leniency, given Sun’s past SEC charges and subsequent pause of investigations.  \
- Experts warn that political ties could polarize sentiment and pose risks, but if executed well, the deal might boost TRX’s institutional profile.

news.headline: DOGE Lord Elon Musk Claims US Runs 'Magic Money Computers'
news.summary:
- Elon Musk, during a podcast with Sen. Ted Cruz, claimed that the U.S. government operates "magic money computers" capable of printing unlimited cash, identifying 14 such computers across various federal agencies.\
- Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) suggests these computers are responsible for sending trillions of dollars to sustain government operations, raising concerns among Bitcoin enthusiasts who view Bitcoin as a hedge against currency devaluation.\
- The term "magic internet money" has historical roots in the Bitcoin community, emphasizing Bitcoin's fixed supply of 21 million BTC as a contrast to government money-printing capabilities.\
- Musk's cost-cutting initiatives have sparked recession fears, with scrutiny over the accuracy of DOGE's reported savings and the transparency of its data.\
- Despite acknowledging some discrepancies in government spending reports, Musk claims the actual spending might be off by up to 10% compared to official figures.

news.headline: Immutable Will Combine Its Ethereum Gaming Networks Into One
news.summary:
- Immutable plans to merge its original Immutable X network with its newer Immutable zkEVM layer-2 blockchain by late 2025, aiming to unify its gaming ecosystem.\
- The zkEVM network has seen significant growth, processing nearly 150 million transactions since its launch in early 2024.\
- The consolidation will automatically migrate assets for users of the Immutable Passport wallet, while other wallet users will have assets transferred to their primary wallet, with an opt-out option available.\
- Game developers will need to adjust as Immutable X APIs will be deprecated, requiring reimplementation of certain mechanics, though asset contracts will maintain ownership structures.\
- The game Guild of Guardians has already completed the migration, with other notable games on the platform including Gods Unchained and Illuvium.\
- Immutable’s IMX token has dropped nearly 25% in the past week, currently priced at $0.393, and briefly fell out of the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market cap.

news.headline: Immutable CSO questions ‘walled garden’ ethos as competition ramps up
news.summary:
- Immutable, a gaming-focused crypto company, manages 500 games and is learning from its extensive experience in the crypto space, according to Chief Studio Officer Justin Hulog.\
- Hulog acknowledges challenges in giving attention to each game and reflects on the mixed success of launches like Guild of Guardians, while celebrating the successful launch of Ravenquest.\
- The company is shifting many games towards its new zkEVM chain, viewing it as the future due to the potential of Layer 2 solutions.\
- Immutable is reconsidering its initial "walled garden" approach, recognizing that a single ecosystem may not suit all games or gamers, especially with the rise of multiple gaming chains.\
- Hulog suggests that instead of competing for dominance, Web3 gaming companies should focus on advancing the industry as a whole to achieve broader adoption and improved user experiences.

PancakeSwap wars: half of CAKE voting power snapped up before new proposal
news.summary:
- PancakeSwap's new proposal aims to eliminate lengthy token lockups for governance participation, targeting a 4% annual deflation rate and replacing the veCAKE and Gauges Voting system with a simpler emissions model.\
- Before the proposal, eight connected addresses acquired and locked 25 million CAKE, securing significant voting power for the upcoming decision.\
- PancakeSwap is the second-largest decentralized exchange on Binance's BNB Chain, with $1.5 billion in assets, according to DeFiLlama.\
- The proposal has sparked debate, with some DeFi projects like Cakepie and StakeDAO opposing it, while some users support the deflationary tokenomics as a potential price driver.\
- Community feedback is mixed, with 150 forum replies expressing concerns over short-term thinking and potential manipulation due to the temporary nature of the token lock.\
- Curve Finance founder Michael Egorov criticized the move, suggesting it undermines the vote-locking governance model and sets a concerning precedent.

news.headline: PancakeSwap Proposes to Reduce CAKE Token Supply by 300 Million
news.summary:
- PancakeSwap, a decentralized crypto exchange, has proposed to reduce the supply of its CAKE token by 300 million.\
- The proposal aims to decrease the maximum supply of CAKE from 750 million to 450 million.\
- Over 99.95% of the community, representing 70,000 votes from CAKE holders, have favored the proposal.\
- If passed, the reduction will be implemented by January 4th, 2024.\
- The PancakeSwap team believes the lower cap will support growth across all deployments and sustain the veCAKE model.\
- CAKE token prices have increased by nearly 10% in the past 24 hours following the proposal.

news.headline: Aave DAO Pushes Back as Interface Fees Shift Away From Treasury
Aave Labs faces backlash over CoW Swap integration
news.summary:
- Aave delegate EzR3aL alleges Aave Labs' integration with CoW Swap diverts excess swap fees, estimated at 15 to 25 basis points, away from the Aave DAO treasury.\
- Previously, swaps via the Aave Labs frontend used ParaSwap, with surplus fees (the last transfer being 46 ETH, over $150,000) flowing to the DAO.\
- Marc Zeller of the Aave Chan Initiative (ACI) labeled the move "stealth privatization," claiming Aave Labs is leveraging DAO-funded assets.\
- Aave Labs argues that CoW Swap integrations exist "entirely outside the protocol the DAO stewards" and that the front-end interface is a product it funds and maintains.\
- Stani Kulechov, Aave founder, stated Aave Labs funded the CoW Swap adapter development internally and asserts the company can monetize its own products.\
- The integration with CoW Swap, announced last week, offers users better prices and MEV protection for various actions like repaying debt or swapping collateral.

news.headline: Verifiable AI took center stage at DAS London with EigenCloud
news.summary:
- Eigen Labs at DAS London highlighted verifiable AI as the next major crypto trend, emphasizing agentic systems with on-chain proof of off-chain work.  \
- Eigen’s stack combines data, compute, and inference services secured by crypto-economic proofs, enabling trust and verifiability before on-chain actions.  \
- Use cases include autonomous trading, agent-to-agent payments, and gaming with attestable outcomes, all relying on programmability and provability.  \
- Trust is identified as the key challenge for AI adoption; solutions involve mechanisms like TEEs, zk proofs, and crypto slashing to ensure verifiable, secure AI operations.  \
- Lit Protocol’s runtime enforcement and EigenCloud’s attestations aim to create a trust stack, with Ethereum positioned as the neutral trust anchor.  \
- Both founders see verifiable AI on Ethereum as a major growth driver, moving from prototypes to production, with the potential for AI-powered financial management in everyday life.

news.headline: Onchain AI agents move from demo to deployment
news.summary:
- Lit Protocol’s Vincent AI agents now support non-custodial, onchain automation with enforceable user-defined policies, moving from demo to early access deployment.  \
- Policies include spend caps, allowlists, and time limits, executed securely within TEEs before any onchain action occurs.  \
- Developers can easily package and enforce app-specific rules via simple SDK calls, enabling well-defined agent actions within set boundaries.  \
- Morpheus highlights that integrating Lit’s MPC-based security features is safer and more battle-tested than custom solutions for managing user funds.  \
- Future plans include privacy-preserving attestations and shared registries for compliance, as well as expanding to credentials, passwords, and multi-chain support beyond DeFi.  \
- The evolution aims to create autonomous, policy-driven agents that manage funds and credentials across various protocols, resembling a “quant in your pocket.”

news.headline: Binance's Former Russia Head, Blum Co-Founder Arrested in Connection to Fraud Case
news.summary:

- Smerkis was detained by local authorities after serving as Binance’s CIS and Russia regional head from 2022 to early 2024.  \
- He co-founded trading app Blum in December 2023 but recently stepped down as chief marketing officer and is no longer involved.  \
- Smerkis has also been managing partner of ventures including Tokenbox.io and The Token Fund.  \
- Binance has not commented on the arrest or the allegations.  \
- The investigation and specific fraud details remain undisclosed.

news.headline: Former Binance Executive and Blum Co-Founder Vladimir Smerkis Arrested in Moscow on May 16 Over $15 Million Fraud
news.summary:

- The criminal investigation involves over $15 million in alleged losses, with specific charges not publicly disclosed.  \
- Smerkis resigned as Blum’s CMO prior to his arrest and has ties to other crypto ventures like Tokenbox and The Token Fond.  \
- He was detained by the Zamoskvoretsky District Court, which ordered his custody.  \
- The case highlights ongoing legal scrutiny in Russia’s crypto sector.

Riot Platforms Bucks Trend of Weak Bitcoin Production in January
news.summary:
- Riot Platforms (RIOT) mined 527 Bitcoin in January, achieving its highest monthly production since December 2023 and a 2% increase from December, bucking the trend of declining production in the sector.\
- The broader bitcoin mining industry saw declines, with MARA Holdings (MARA) mining 750 BTC, a 13% drop, and Cleanspark (CLSK) mining 626 BTC, a 6% decrease.\
- Other firms like IREN, Core Scientific (CORZ), Cipher Mining (CIFR), Bitfarms (BITF), and Hut 8 (HUT) also reported declines ranging from 2% to 31%.\
- The decline in production is largely attributed to increased network difficulty, which adjusts every 2,016 blocks, with the next adjustment expected to reach a record high.\
- Despite the challenges, Cipher Mining (CIFR) has seen a 27% increase in stock performance year-to-date, while IREN, RIOT, and CLSK have also posted double-digit gains.\
- Some companies, including Hive (HIVE), Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR), and TerraWulf (WULF), have not yet reported their January production figures.

news.summary:

- Reddit ended its involvement with the MOON token in November last year, but moderators continue to manage it independently.\
- The competition aims to create a new logo that looks similar to the old one, with minimal noticeable changes.\
- Moderators clarified that the new logo must adhere to Reddit’s brand guidelines and cannot use AI-generated images.\
- The token’s price is currently $0.1146, up 235% since its crash in October 2023, but down 83% from its all-time high in March 2023.\

news.headline: Reddit Community Token MOON Hits Record High Ahead of Celer's Multidirectional Bridge Launch
news.summary:

- Interoperability protocol Celer will support MOON, allowing token holders to bridge their coins to Arbitrum One starting March 20.\
- MOON's rally is attributed to the upcoming launch of Celer's bidirectional bridging facility.\
- MOON is an ERC-20 token distributed as rewards on the r/CryptoCurrency subreddit.\
- The bridge will enable users to move MOON tokens between Arbitrum Nova and Arbitrum One.\
- Bridging MOON to Arbitrum One will provide exposure to a wider range of DeFi applications.\

news.headline: Linea reaffirms decentralization plan after deliberately pausing block production amid Velocore hack
news.summary:
- Ethereum Layer 2 blockchain Linea plans to decentralize its network after deliberately pausing block production in response to a security exploit on Velocore, a decentralized exchange on the network.\
- The exploit led to an unauthorized withdrawal of over $7 million in ether on June 2.\
- Linea halted block production to prevent the hacker from converting stolen tokens into ether.\
- Industry figures, including Matter Labs' CEO, emphasized the importance of decentralizing Layer 2 networks.\
- Linea acknowledged that its network is still centralized but is actively working towards a decentralized model.\
- Decentralizing the network would eliminate single points of failure and ensure fairness in block sequencing.

news.headline: Decentralized exchange Velocore addresses $7 million hack in postmortem, offers bounty to hacker
news.summary:
- Decentralized exchange Velocore experienced a $6.8 million hack through a vulnerability in its smart contracts.\
- The hacker exploited an overflow logic vulnerability to trick Velocore into turning a small withdrawal into a large deposit.\
- The hacker used a flash loan to drain Velocore's "volatile pools" on zkSync Era and Linea blockchains.\
- Velocore has disabled the logic flaw and offered a 10% white hat bounty to the hacker for the return of the remaining funds.\
- The Linea Ethereum Layer 2 network temporarily paused block production in an unsuccessful attempt to mitigate losses.\
- Velocore plans to compensate affected users and has taken a snapshot of the blockchain state prior to the incident.

news.headline: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov ‘instilled optimism’ with vow to clean up app as Toncoin jumps 7%
news.summary:

- Toncoin (TON), the cryptocurrency associated with Telegram, surged nearly 7% to $4.89 after Durov's statement, rebounding from a 28% decline since his arrest.\
- Durov emphasized that Telegram actively removes millions of harmful posts and channels daily, countering claims of the app being an "anarchic paradise."\
- Analysts, including Ajay Dhingra from Unizen and Hong Yea from GRVT, attribute Toncoin's recent price surge to Durov's commitment to cleaning up the platform.\
- Bitcoin and Ethereum have both fallen 2.2% over the last 24 hours, trading at $55,796 and $2,349 respectively.\
- Toncoin is currently trading at $4.78, slightly down from its recent high of $4.89.

news.headline: How high will Solana go? Four experts say this will drive the price
news.summary:

- BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes predicts Solana could hit $250 if Bitcoin and Ether break above $70,000 and $4,000, driven by potential liquidity from US Treasury actions.\
- Placeholder’s Chris Burniske forecasts Solana reaching $1,000, citing its popularity among large-cap crypto investors.\
- Unizen’s Ajay Dhingra highlights strong institutional interest, with examples like Hamilton Lane’s private credit fund and VanEck’s Solana ETF filing.\

news.headline: From Bybit to GMX: The 10 biggest crypto hacks of 2025
news.summary:
- Total crypto thefts in 2025 across the 10 biggest incidents reached an estimated $2.2 billion, matching the total stolen in 2024.\
- The largest crypto theft ever recorded occurred in February 2025 when Bybit suffered a $1.4 billion breach, with approximately 401,000 ETH drained from Safe-based multisig wallets.\
- Cetus experienced a $223 million exploit in May 2025 due to manipulated pricing from spoofed tokens, while Balancer V2 lost $128 million in November 2025 due to a rounding-error bug in composable stable pools.\
- Centralized exchanges Phemex and Nobitex lost $85 million (Jan. 23) and $80 million to $90 million (June 18) respectively, both incidents pointing toward compromised hot wallet private keys.\
- Bitget lost $100 million in April 2025 by exploiting flaws in the exchange's internal trading bot logic concerning the VOXEL market.\
- GMX sustained a $42 million loss in July 2025 targeting its V1 system on Arbitrum via a reentrancy-style vulnerability in a contract linked to the GLP liquidity pool.

news.headline: Bitget will legally pursue 8 accounts suspected of profiting $20 million from VOXEL trading manipulation
news.summary:
- Bitget plans to legally pursue eight accounts suspected of manipulating its VOXEL market, which led to over $20 million in illicit profits.  \
- The suspicious activity occurred around April 20, when VOXEL's price surged over 500% in a short period.  \
- The platform has temporarily rolled back trades and is investigating "abnormal trading" linked to the price spike.  \
- Bitget's Head of Asia, Xie Jiayin, stated that lawyer's letters will be sent to the account owners as a precursor to legal action.  \
- Recovered funds will be redistributed to users via an airdrop, with a full incident report forthcoming.  \
- This incident adds to recent crypto exploits, including a $5.8M Solana DeFi hack and a $49M stablecoin loss from Infini.

news.headline: Recent Altcoin Rally Powered by South Korean Traders, CryptoQuant Says
news.summary:
- South Korean traders are driving the recent altcoin rally, according to CryptoQuant.\
- Altcoins have experienced rapid price surges in the past few months, partly due to South Korean traders' high-risk behaviors.\
- Spot volumes on local exchange Upbit have nearly doubled since September, attracting market makers and traders.\
- Notable altcoin booms include Loom Network's LOOM and HIFI, which saw significant price increases.\
- Bitcoin's market dominance has increased to 53%, suggesting it remains the preferred choice among traders.\
- Overall market capitalization has risen to $1.4 trillion from just over $1 trillion since September.

news.headline: First Mover Americas: Friend.tech Drives Up Coinbase’s Base Blockchain Activity
news.summary:
- Coinbase's layer 2 blockchain, Base, has reached an all-time high in daily transactions, surpassing layer 2 rivals Arbitrum and Optimism combined.\
- The surge in Base's activity is driven by the decentralized social network platform Friend.tech, which is built on Base and saw its daily transactions also reach an all-time high.\
- Coinbase states that the sale of tokens held by bankrupt crypto exchange FTX will not cause a market shock due to limited liquidations and gradual increases in weekly sales.\
- The U.S. Department of Justice finds FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried's proposed jury questions "unnecessarily intrusive" and potentially supportive of his defense.\
- HIFI, the native token of Polygon-based project Hifi Finance, experienced a significant surge followed by a drop after being listed on Binance.

news.headline: HyperLiquid unlocks 12M HYPE tokens, dilutes holders by $330M
news.summary:
- HyperLiquid unlocked 12,457,813 HYPE tokens from a founding allocation today, diluting token holders by $331 million.\
- The circulating supply and market cap of HYPE instantly increased by 5.5%, moving the supply from 225,927,502 to 238,385,315 tokens.\
- The HYPE token has lost 36% of its value over the past six months, with a 6% decrease in the last month leading up to this unlock.\
- A subsequent unlock of approximately $268 million worth of tokens is scheduled for February 6, 2026.\
- Combining the November 29, 2025, the current unlock, and the February unlock equates to $945 million in dilution for HYPE holders in under three months.\

- CAR’s meme coin surged initially in February, reaching a peak market cap of $884M, but has since fallen 98% from its all-time high.  \
- Other country-led meme coins, like Trump’s and Argentina’s LIBRA, experienced massive hype followed by sharp declines, showing limited lasting power.  \
- Despite recent gains, CAR’s meme coin remains far below its peak, highlighting the volatility and fleeting nature of country-backed tokens.  \
- The article suggests CAR may be attempting to legitimize meme coins through potential legislation, unlike previous failed attempts.

Mastercard Joins Forces With Mercuryo To Launch Non-Custodial Crypto Card in Europe
news.summary:
- Mastercard has partnered with Mercuryo to launch a non-custodial crypto debit card called Spend in Europe.\
- The Spend card allows users to pay with 40 cryptocurrencies at over 100 million merchants using their non-custodial wallets.\
- The card integrates with Apple Pay and Google Pay, offering automatic fiat-to-crypto conversion, though full integration with crypto providers may take up to a week.\
- Users must complete Know-Your-Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) verification processes to use the card.\
- The card has a $1.78 (€1.60) issuance fee, a $1.11 (€1) monthly maintenance fee, and a $44,000 monthly spending limit.\
- Spend is not the first crypto Mastercard; the company has also partnered with Tap, Coinbase, and Nexo for similar products.

news.headline: Why UK Crypto Manager Tap Global Is Entering the US—Despite an Uncertain Regulatory Environment
news.summary:
- Tap Global, a UK-based crypto management company, has expanded its operations to the United States, offering trading access to multiple digital assets.\
- Despite concerns about regulatory uncertainty in the US, Tap Global believes the risk is minimized through its partnership with Zero Hash, a Chicago-based infrastructure platform.\
- Zero Hash has taken steps to stay compliant with regulators and Tap Global will operate under their regulatory cover.\
- By entering the US market, Tap Global gains access to the estimated 93 million Americans who currently own cryptocurrency.\
- Tap Global plans to use this expansion as a catalyst for further geographic expansion, including Canada and Latin America.\
- The company aims to establish itself as a trusted actor by demonstrating its "regulation first approach" in the US market.

news.headline: Sei blockchain to add Ethereum Virtual Machine support in V2 upgrade
news.summary: Sei Labs, the team behind the Sei blockchain, is planning to add support for the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) in its upcoming V2 upgrade. This upgrade will allow developers to port Ethereum-based contracts onto the Sei network and use them. To enable this feature, Sei V2 will incorporate Geth, a widely used Ethereum client software. Sei Labs has developed a specialized interface to facilitate interaction between Sei's existing smart contracts component (based on CosmWasm technology) and the EVM component. The upgrade also introduces SeiDB, a data structure aimed at improving the platform's storage layer and boosting overall performance and scalability. The Sei V2 upgrade is nearing completion and is expected to be released on a public testnet in Q1 2024, with the mainnet deployment scheduled for H1 2024. Sei is a Cosmos SDK-based proof-of-stake blockchain designed for trading with sub-second block execution.

Nearly 70% of Base Is Used By Project of ‘Questionable’ Utility
news.summary:

- XEN is a virtual mining platform where users can mint XEN and XENFT tokens on various networks, including Base.\
- Users have been taking advantage of Base's low fees to mint new XEN and XENFT tokens in large quantities.\
- This issue of resource monopolization by a small number of users has implications for other cheap fee blockchains as well.\
- XEN has previously caused gas price spikes on Ethereum and is currently the largest contract on the network.\
- The report suggests repricing storage as a solution to prevent resource monopolization.

Aleph Zero launches crypto privacy app for shielded transactions on EVM chains like Arbitrum
news.summary:

- The app breaks the link between public wallet activity and transactions, supporting shielded asset transfers, yield, staking rewards, and bridging.  \
- Built with Aleph Zero’s infrastructure and Common, a privacy-focused DeFi project, it uses zero-knowledge cryptography for fast, private transactions.  \
- Supports wallets like MetaMask, Ledger, and Rabby, with a mobile version expected by late May.  \
- The system ensures compliance and auditability by preserving provenance while anonymizing activity with subsecond proof times.  \
- Aleph Zero’s Layer 1 blockchain leverages a bespoke consensus protocol and DAG cryptography for scalability, speed, and privacy.

news.headline: Interchain Foundation backs Stride to build IBC-native DEX on Cosmos Hub
news.summary:

- The initiative aims to enhance cross-chain liquidity and interoperability within the Cosmos ecosystem.  \
- Stride's platform will leverage the Inter-Blockchain Communication (IBC) protocol for seamless asset transfers.  \

- The article also mentions Aleph Zero launching a privacy app for shielded transactions on EVM-compatible chains like Arbitrum.

news.summary:

news.headline: Crypto Gaming Platform Portal Adds Rockstar Co-Founder Amid Airdrop Push
news.summary:

- Portal is a planned "universal" gaming token that aims to create a seamless cross-chain crypto gaming experience using the LayerZero interoperability protocol.\
- King will work roughly four days per month on the project.\
- Over 200 blockchain games have agreed to accept the Portal token as a game currency when it launches.\
- The Portal team has launched a gamified social token-farming experience called Crystal Dash for users to earn points towards the upcoming Portal token airdrop.\
- Portal has gained support from projects like Crypto Unicorns, Space Nation Online, Nine Chronicles, and Magic Eden.

news.headline: Uniswap Labs pays $175K to settle CFTC charges
news.summary:
- Uniswap Labs settled with the CFTC for $175K over alleged violations of the Commodity Exchange Act.\
- The settlement does not require Uniswap to admit or deny the allegations.\
- The CFTC's order claims Uniswap's interface was used to trade tokens representing leveraged exposure to digital assets like ether and bitcoin.\
- Tokens involved include BTC2XFLI, ETH2XFLI, ETH2XFLI-P, BTC2XFLI-P, and oSQTH, issued by Index.\
- The CFTC argues that offering these assets without ensuring users were eligible contract participants violated the Commodity Exchange Act.\
- The fine was reduced due to Uniswap's substantial cooperation and remediation efforts, representing just 0.1% of its raised funds.

news.headline: Index Coop RWA basket builds on hyETH experiment
news.summary:
- Index Coop has introduced a Real World Asset (RWA) Index Token to provide easy access to real-world assets in DeFi.\
- The index is developed in partnership with Fortunafi and includes projects like Maker, Ondo Finance, Ethena, Centrifuge, Canto, and Maple Finance.\
- Product Revenue Tokens (PRTs) are being used to attract early investors, entitling them to a share of the revenue generated by the RWA Index.\
- The success of the presale will determine if the RWA index will launch.\
- Index Coop aims to incentivize behaviors that add value to the product, such as market liquidity and integrations with other DeFi platforms.\
- Initially, both RWA and hyETH (another Index Coop product) are on Ethereum mainnet, but they are expected to expand to layer-2 solutions.

Namada blockchain completes mainnet rollout for composable privacy layer as native token set to begin trading
news.summary:
- Namada, a Cosmos-based privacy-focused Layer 1 blockchain, has completed its mainnet rollout, introducing a composable privacy layer for decentralized apps.  \
- The launch coincides with a free-market token distribution, with NAM token trading expected to start this week on various exchanges.  \
- The network features a multi-asset shielded pool enabling private transactions and asset concealment within decentralized exchange liquidity pools.  \
- Namada plans to expand cross-chain support, including Bitcoin via Lombard and Babylon, Ethereum, and Solana through bridges, plus shielded swaps on Osmosis.  \
- Users will be able to perform shielded DeFi, gaming, and payments activities, with cross-chain swaps offering increased privacy and simplicity.  \
- The project has raised $60 million from investors like Polychain Capital and CMCC, with ongoing integrations to enhance cross-chain privacy capabilities.

news.summary:

- The rally is driven by increasing concerns over financial surveillance, sanctions, and geopolitical uncertainty, prompting investors to seek privacy-focused assets.  \
- Other privacy coins such as Veil Token (+248%), Railgun (+225%), PIVX (+106%), and BEAM (+73%) also saw significant gains; Monero remained flat.  \
- Grayscale launched a Zcash trust, and Railgun’s total value locked more than doubled, signaling rising user activity and momentum.  \
- Experts note that privacy coins tend to rally during times of economic uncertainty and increased surveillance fears, with institutional and retail investor interest growing.  \
- Bitcoin pulled back slightly to around $121,349 after hitting nearly $125,000 over the weekend, as markets digest the U.S. government shutdown.

news.headline: Getting Started with Veil's Privacy Protocol on Base
news.summary:
- Veil is a privacy app on the Base network that uses zk-SNARKs to allow users to move ETH or USDC without creating links between wallets, enhancing transaction privacy.\
- Users can deposit funds into either Public or Verified Pools, with Verified Pools requiring Coinbase verification and a minimum of 2,500 $VEIL tokens to participate.\
- Upon deposit, Veil generates a "secret note" containing a cryptographic proof and a withdrawal key, which is essential for future withdrawals.\
- The $VEIL token is central to the protocol, offering rewards for participation and staking, with a 0.5% fee on deposits contributing to staking rewards.\
- To use Veil, users must connect their wallet, select a pool, generate and secure their secret note, and confirm transactions for both deposits and withdrawals.\
- Privacy tips include waiting before withdrawing, using active pools, breaking up large deposits, avoiding predictable behavior, and withdrawing to new wallets to maintain anonymity.

news.headline: Crypto Markets Today: Largest tokens decline, with derivatives signaling caution ahead
news.summary:
- Risk-off sentiment drove Bitcoin back toward $88,000, leading the broader CoinDesk 20 (CD20) index down 2.9% over the last 24 hours.\
- Gold surged to record highs above $5,500 an ounce, absorbing crypto outflows, while Bitcoin traded more like a liquidity-sensitive risk asset than a hedge.\
- Cumulative crypto futures open interest dropped nearly 3% to $132.26 billion, with $348.30 million in mostly bullish long bets liquidated.\
- Optimism's community approved a plan to use over half of the Superchain's revenue (estimated at $17M+ last year) for monthly OP token buybacks starting in February, pending a final protocol vote.\
- Investors filed a $100 million federal lawsuit against Cere Network insiders, alleging fraud and token-dumping immediately following its 2021 ICO.\
- Pudgy Penguins is executing a strategy to transition into a multi-vertical consumer IP platform utilizing phygital goods, games, and the PENGU token.

news.headline: Investors accuse Cere Network of $100 million fraud and token-dump scheme in lawsuit
news.summary:
- Investors filed a $100 million federal lawsuit against Cere Network founder Fred Jin and other insiders, alleging fraud stemming from the project's November 2021 initial coin offering (ICO).\
- The complaint claims executives misled backers regarding token lockups, customer adoption, and technical readiness while insiders sold tens of millions of dollars in tokens immediately after launch.\
- Lead plaintiffs Lujunjin “Vivian” Liu and Goopal Digital Ltd. are seeking $25 million in compensatory damages and $75 million in punitive damages.\
- Cere Network raised nearly $50 million through token sales, with investors allegedly promised major exchange listings for the CERE token.\
- The lawsuit contends that insiders dumped tokens post-launch, causing CERE to collapse from $0.45 at launch to near $0.0012, a drop exceeding 99%.\
- Plaintiffs argue proceeds from token sales were used to enrich insiders rather than fund infrastructure buildout, citing misleading claims about Fortune 1000 clients.

news.headline: Can ‘Code’ open up Solana’s closed culture?
news.summary:
- Solana-based crypto wallet, Code, has open-sourced its code using the MIT License.\
- This makes Code one of the first Solana-based applications to fully open-source its software.\
- The MIT License allows users to freely use, copy, modify, and distribute Code's software.\
- Code's move aligns with Satoshi's vision of a peer-to-peer electronic cash system.\
- Many apps on the Solana blockchain are traditionally built privately, but Code's open-sourcing can lead to more leadership in the ecosystem.\
- Code was founded by the team behind Kik and its cryptocurrency, Kin.

Bitcoin Braces for First Inflation Test Since US Shutdown
news.summary:
- Bitcoin and markets are awaiting U.S. inflation data post-government shutdown, with analysts expecting a muted market reaction.  \
- The Consumer Price Index (CPI) release on October 25 will influence the Federal Reserve’s upcoming interest rate decision amid economic uncertainty.  \
- Consensus forecasts a 3.1% inflation rise, but independent data from Truflation suggests a lower 2.28%, with expectations of a modest or flat reading.  \
- Focus remains on employment and trade tensions, with recent tariffs and strong labor market signals reducing the likelihood of a major market move from inflation data.  \
- Bitcoin has fallen 2.5% to around $107,000, trading below its October 10 high, while traditional equities like the S&P 500 remain near recent peaks.  \
- Investor sentiment is cautious, with increased hedging and lower implied volatility in options, reflecting risk aversion ahead of key economic data.

news.headline: Crypto Daybook Americas: Roses Are Red, Violets Are Blue, Inflation Is Hot, but Bitcoin Shines Through
news.summary:

- Analysts suggest Bitcoin's resilience may be due to expectations of easing inflation, with the Truflation U.S. Inflation Index indicating a potential decline to 2.06%.\
- Historical trends and the 200-week moving average suggest the Bitcoin bull market could continue, with short-term holders accumulating 1.5 million BTC since September.\
- On the corporate front, Coinbase reported strong earnings, and Gamestop is considering a Bitcoin investment, potentially boosting market sentiment.\
- Upcoming crypto events include network upgrades for Dynamic TAO and Qtum, and Ethereum's Pectra upgrade testing, alongside significant token unlocks for Starknet, Sei, Arbitrum, and others.\
- In the broader market, Bitcoin's dominance is slightly down, while Ethereum and other major indices like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq have seen gains.

news.headline: VIRTUAL Rallies Ahead of First Ethereum-Based AI Agent Launch
news.summary:
- Virtuals Protocol’s VIRTUAL token rose 7% ahead of the launch of I.R.I.S., its first Ethereum-based AI agent, which is oversubscribed by over 1500%.  \
- I.R.I.S., a collaboration with Nethermind, introduces AuditAgent for real-time smart contract security analysis and social media monitoring.  \
- The IRIS Genesis launch marks a major ecosystem milestone, expanding from Base to Ethereum, with potential future expansion to blockchains like Aptos.  \
- VIRTUAL’s price is $2.20, down from its January high of $5.07, with a market cap of $1.3 billion, boosted by a recent 130% weekly surge.  \
- VaderAI, an autonomous hedge fund AI agent, expanded from Virtuals Protocol to VaderAI on June 9, with Yapping points now earning users additional rewards.  \
- VADER tokens have surged 71% over the past week, and Vader Yappers can earn staking rewards and airdrops by engaging on social media and staking tokens.

news.headline: Solana Meme Coins TRUMP, VINE See Double Digit Gains in Market Lull
news.summary:
- Solana meme coins TRUMP and VINE have seen significant gains, with TRUMP rising 12.4% and VINE climbing 35.4% in the past 24 hours, despite a market lull among top cryptocurrencies.\
- The TRUMP token, endorsed by the U.S. President, rebounded after hitting its lowest price since launch, following controversial political comments by Trump and a 75% drop from its all-time high due to trade tensions.\
- Fartboy (FARTBOY), another Solana meme coin, surged 66.8% in a day, possibly linked to U.S. National Fart Day celebrations, while Vader AI (VADER) and Dogwifhat (WIF) also saw gains.\

news.headline: $$B3 Token Launches as Layer 3 Solution on Base Blockchain, Trade on Bybit with 140 Million Prize Pool
news.summary:
- The $B3 token has been launched as a Layer 3 solution on the Base blockchain, which is associated with Coinbase, focusing on the gaming sector.\
- Developed by former Coinbase employees, B3 aims to create a robust gaming ecosystem with a native currency and user rewards across various games.\
- The token is now available for trading on Bybit, with a prize pool of 140 million $B3 tokens for traders.\
- $B3 can also be traded on Base using platforms like Aerodrome and AlienBase, with more exchanges expected to join soon.

news.headline: 'Forgotten Runiverse' on Ethereum Network Ronin Is the Latest Crypto Gaming Casualty
news.summary:
- Forgotten Runiverse, an MMORPG on the Ronin network, is taking its game servers offline until further notice due to financial infeasibility.\
- The team behind the game, linked to the Forgotten Runes NFT collection, stated that maintaining the game live in its current state is no longer financially viable.\
- All account and progress data will be preserved securely, though the free-to-play, browser-based game servers will shut down on Tuesday, January 28, 2026.\
- This closure reflects a broader trend in the crypto gaming sector, following the shutdowns of titles like Deadrop, Nyan Heroes, and Pirate Nation last year.\
- Forgotten Runiverse had previously secured funding from investors including Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian and had approval for development across Nintendo, PlayStation, and Xbox.\
- The developers maintain the project is not abandoned, referring to the pause as the "Runiverse isn’t gone, merely resting."

UK Just Passed an Online Safety Bill That Will Apply to the Metaverse
news.summary:
- UK lawmakers have passed an Online Safety Bill that will also apply to the metaverse.\
- The bill requires companies to assess the likelihood of encountering illegal content and harmful content for children.\
- Companies must develop ways to mitigate these risks, aiming to boost both freedom and safety online.\
- The inclusion of the metaverse in the bill is seen as necessary due to its growth and Facebook's rebranding to Meta.\
- The bill will need approval from King Charles and will come into force immediately unless a later date is set.

news.headline: Funding Roundup: A Web3 recruiting network announces $10M raise
news.summary:
- Web3 recruiting network Bondex has raised $10 million in funding rounds.\
- Venture capitalist firms Animoca Brands, Morningstar, and Chainlink participated in the funding.\
- Bondex aims to shift incentives and demand away from middlemen and empower users in the professional networking model.\
- The platform gamifies recruitment by offering token or USD bounties for successful hires.\
- The funding will be used to enhance the platform and integrate it with Web3 and Web2 ecosystems.\
- Binance, Chainlink, and Blockchain.com are among the companies using Bondex for hiring.

news.headline: Crypto-fueled LinkedIn rival Bondex says it’s raised over $10 million
news.summary:
- Bondex, a crypto-fueled LinkedIn rival, has raised over $10 million in funding.\
- The funding includes $4 million from investors like Animoca Brands and Morningstar, as well as $6.5 million from a community sale on CoinList.\
- Bondex aims to be a gamified version of LinkedIn, powered by its BDXN token.\
- Users on the platform can earn referral bounties of up to $10,000 for successful hires.\
- The majority of the raised capital will be used to improve the platform and compete with traditional professional networking sites.\
- Binance, Blockchain.com, Chainlink, CoinMarketCap, and Ankr have joined as recruiting partners.

news.headline: Hyperliquid \u0026 The Year Perps Caught Fire
news.summary:
- Hyperliquid ranked 4th across all of crypto for revenue in 2025, generating over $650M, and briefly commanded 70% of onchain perps volume in May.\
- The platform listed the TRUMP token almost immediately upon launch in January 2025, cementing its reputation for fast listings of new assets.\
- The HyperEVM, launched in February 2025, grew Total Value Locked (TVL) from $350M in April to $1.8B by mid-June without relying on token incentive programs.\
- In Q3 2025, Hyperliquid integrated with Phantom, Rabby, and MetaMask via builder codes, routing $158B in volume and earning partners nearly $50M in fees.\
- Native Markets won the USDH stablecoin stewardship RFP over competitors like Ethena and Paxos, despite being less capitalized, aligning with Hyperliquid’s bootstrapped ethos.\
- HYPE price dropped nearly 50% from its mid-September peak following an Automatic De-Leveraging (ADL) event in October and the start of team token unlocks in November.

news.headline: Trump Tokens Outperform After US President Teases ‘Tariff Dividends’
news.summary:

- The rally follows news that the U.S. government may reopen soon, with President Trump hinting at stimulus checks funded by tariff revenue.  \
- Trump posted on Truth Social claiming tariffs have made the U.S. the "Richest, Most Respected Country," with a potential $2,000 dividend per person, though Treasury officials suggested it could be in the form of tax cuts.  \

- The TRUMP memecoin, launched just before Trump’s inauguration, previously soared from $400 million to $75 billion in 72 hours but has since declined to a $9 billion valuation.  \
- Bitcoin rose just over 1% today, while the altcoins led the market’s positive momentum.

news.headline: Retardio memecoin ‘not authorised’ by UK FCA
news.summary:

- Retardio responded to the FCA's warning with a meme of Jeff Bezos in clown makeup.\

- The FCA is seeking industry feedback on new crypto regulations and has expanded its cryptocurrency-focused staff to over 100 members, with the policy team growing the fastest.\

news.summary:
- Flowcarbon, co-founded by WeWork's Adam Neumann, is refunding holders of its "Goddess Nature Token" (GNT) after a failed launch.\
- The startup, launched in 2022, raised $70 million, including $38 million from GNT sales, to tokenize carbon credits on the blockchain.\
- The refund decision follows market instability and resistance from carbon registries, with Flowcarbon citing industry delays.\
- CEO Dana Gibber previously paused the token launch in July 2022 due to market conditions and warnings from carbon credit registry Verra.\
- The traditional carbon credit market, valued at over $330 billion in 2022, remains lucrative, with ongoing efforts to enhance transparency through blockchain technology.

news.headline: Toucan Protocol with Raphaël Haupt | Green Pill #30
Aave DAO clashes with founder over website revenue
news.summary:
- Aave founder Stani Kulechov and Aave Labs clashed with the Aave DAO community over the privatization of interface swap revenue.\
- The controversy began when Aave Labs partnered with CoWSwap for the user interface, diverting swap revenue from the DAO to Aave Labs.\
- Ezr3al, the DAO's largest delegate, noted the new CoWSwap revenue is annualized at over $10 million, significantly higher than the $1.1 million generated by the previous provider, Velora, in 2025.\
- Aave Labs defended its actions, stating it owns the interface and the fee collection is reasonable to cover interface operating costs.\
- Marc Zeller, founder of the Aave Chan Initiative, argued Aave Labs has a fiduciary duty to the cooperative, implying a tacit agreement regarding interface revenue sharing.\
- In response to the dispute, at least three governance proposals were submitted to the DAO forum, including one seeking full ownership of Aave Labs' equity and intellectual property.

news.headline: ParaSwap to end PSP utility as Velora rebrand shifts governance to VLR token
news.summary:
- Velora, formerly ParaSwap, launches its new VLR token to replace PSP, ending its governance, staking, and rewards utility immediately.  \
- PSP remains transferable and usable but will no longer be officially supported; migration to VLR is open for at least a year with bonus rewards for early migration by Dec. 16.  \
- VLR introduces gasless migration, unified staking on Base, and revenue-linked rewards, aiming to simplify and align incentives with protocol usage.  \
- The transition concludes Project Miró, a DAO-led initiative to overhaul Velora’s branding, governance, and token model for long-term sustainability.  \
- Velora has processed over $125 billion in trading volume since 2019, with a peak monthly volume of over $7 billion in August.  \
- Velora emphasizes transparency and community alignment as it shifts toward a more sustainable DeFi infrastructure.

news.headline: Liberland delays LLD bridge to Justin Sun’s TRON for second time
news.summary:
- Liberland has postponed the launch of its LLD token bridge to Justin Sun’s TRON blockchain for the second time, citing unspecified reasons for the delay.\
- The initial delay was due to developers' concerns about deploying on a Friday, with the launch now pushed to a later date.\
- The bridge's delay raises potential conflict of interest issues, as Justin Sun, TRON's founder, is also the prime minister of Liberland.\
- Sun's involvement in Liberland's international relations includes plans to strengthen ties with the US, though reports suggest he may not have attended key events like Trump's inauguration.\
- Sun faces legal challenges in the US, including charges of fraud and wash trading, but proceedings are currently paused as he seeks a resolution.\
- Last year, Sun purchased $75 million in crypto tokens from Donald Trump’s World Liberty Financial following Trump's election.

news.headline: Justin Sun Gets Elected Prime Minister of the “Freest Country” in the World
news.summary:
- **Justin Sun Elected**: Tron founder Justin Sun has been elected as the Prime Minister of Liberland, a self-proclaimed libertarian micronation located between Croatia and Serbia.\
- **Blockchain Voting**: The election utilized a blockchain-based voting system, which aims to provide transparency and eliminate doubts about election outcomes.\
- **Governance and Tokens**: Liberland operates entirely on-chain using the Polkadot Virtual Machine and employs a dual-token system: LLD (Liberland Dollar) for operational purposes and LLM (Liberland Merit) for governance.\
- **Population and Citizenship**: Liberland has around 1,000 citizens, 4,000 e-residents, and over 700,000 applicants for citizenship. Naturalization requires spending more than 25 days in the territory.\
- **Future Plans**: Sun aims to build upon libertarian principles and sees Liberland as the heart of the libertarian movement, similar to how Vatican City serves Catholics. The nation is also drafting its constitution for citizen approval.

news.headline: Avalanche Active Addresses Spike 140%
news.summary:
- Avalanche's active addresses surged by 140%, reaching 360,000 unique wallets on March 4, up from 150,000 a week prior.\
- The increase in activity is attributed to memecoin presales and token airdrops, particularly the BLUB token, which saw significant trading volume and price appreciation.\
- Avalanche's largest decentralized exchange, LFJ (formerly Trader Joe), reported a 35% increase in trading volume, processing $580 million over the last week.\
- The BLUB token launched with a $50 million market cap, peaking at $110 million, and generated nearly $30 million in trading volume.\
- Despite the rise in activity, the AVAX token price saw minimal impact, trading at $21.45, up 6% in the last 24 hours but remaining flat over the week.

Arbitrum Blockchain Traders Can Now Protect Against 'Impermanent Loss'
news.summary:
- GammaSwap has launched on the Arbitrum network, offering protection against impermanent loss for liquidity providers.\
- GammaSwap allows DeFi users to borrow LP tokens from AMMs and "short" them, creating low-risk trading strategies.\
- Impermanent loss occurs when the ratio between tokens in a pool changes, resulting in a loss for liquidity providers.\
- GammaSwap allows traders to "go long" volatility by effectively "shorting" LP tokens, creating an impermanent gain.\
- This feature could encourage more users to become liquidity providers and increase liquidity across Arbitrum.\
- GammaSwap plans to deploy on more blockchains and provide support for Uniswap LPs in the future.

news.headline: ‘Hawk Tuah’ star pulled into expanding memecoin lawsuit
'Hawk Tuah Girl' Hailey Welch Claims SEC, FBI Cleared Her for the HAWK Memecoin Disaster
news.summary:
- Haley Welch, aka “Hawk Tuah Girl,” claims she was cleared by the SEC and FBI after being questioned over the failed HAWK memecoin launched on Solana in December 2024.  \
- Welch says authorities went through her phone and found no wrongdoing, but she now distances herself from the project, expressing regret and claiming she didn’t fully understand crypto.  \
- The HAWK token, which briefly reached a $491 million market cap, plummeted below $100 million within hours; Welch’s team said it was legally compliant and backed by a Cayman foundation.  \
- She estimates user losses at $180,000, down from initial estimates of $1.2 million, though many holders never sold, so actual losses could be higher.  \
- Critics on her podcast accuse Welch of endorsing the project without understanding it, highlighting her lack of crypto knowledge.  \
- As of May 21, 2025, HAWK’s market cap has fallen 99% since December, now valued at around $104,000.

news.headline: DeFi Lenders Processed Nearly $300 Million of Liquidations on Feb. 3
news.summary:
- On February 3, DeFi lenders processed nearly $300 million in liquidations, with Aave, Compound, and Sky liquidating $207.84 million, $78 million, and $7.61 million, respectively.\
- ETH liquidations on Compound and Aave totaled approximately $140.78 million, followed by wBTC with $45 million, marking the largest daily liquidations on Compound and the second largest on Aave V3.\
- The liquidations were handled without incurring bad debt, indicating the maturity of the DeFi ecosystem.\
- CoinGlass reported over $2.37 billion in leveraged crypto positions liquidated on the same day, but Bybit CEO Ben Zhou suggested the actual figure could be between $8-10 billion.\
- Zhou noted that exchanges, including Bybit, report limited data to Coinglass due to API restrictions but promised increased transparency by pushing all liquidation data.

news.headline: Bitcoin Suisse, Steakhouse Financial and Block Analitica join as validators for oracle provider Chronicle Protocol
news.summary:
- Bitcoin Suisse, Steakhouse Financial, and Block Analitica have joined Chronicle Protocol's decentralized validator network to enhance security and resilience against potential oracle hacks or failures.\
- Chronicle Protocol secures about 23% of the total value locked on Ethereum, making its security crucial for the DeFi ecosystem.\
- The protocol relies on a trust model where no more than 50% of validators can be malicious, and it enhances security by inviting reputable validators.\
- Chronicle is a key oracle provider for major DeFi projects like Sky, Anemoy, and M^0, and was initially part of MakerDAO’s oracle team.\
- Its blockchain-agnostic tool, Scribe, uses Schnorr signatures to reduce gas costs and includes fraud proofs for disputing prices and malicious acts.\
- The protocol secures over $8 billion in funds, with significant contributions from MakerDAO and Spark, and is supported by validators like Infura, Etherscan, and dYdX.

news.headline: Gate Unveils Layer 2 Network and Tokenomics Overhaul for GT Token
news.summary:
- Gate has launched Gate Layer, a high-performance Layer 2 blockchain built on OP Stack and fully compatible with Ethereum, aiming to increase transaction throughput and lower costs.  \
- The upgrade makes GT token the exclusive gas token for Gate Layer, continuing its dual burn model, with over 180 million GT burned to date (about 60% of initial supply).  \
- This move aligns with a broader industry trend, as major exchanges like Coinbase with Base are developing their own Layer 2 solutions to boost onchain activity and ecosystem growth.  \
- Gate is positioning Gate Layer as the backbone of its “All in Web3” strategy, supporting tools such as Perp (perpetuals hub), Gate Fun (no-code token incubator), and Meme Go (cross-chain meme tracker).  \
- The company aims to deepen liquidity, expand its ecosystem, and establish itself as a full-stack Web3 provider, not just a trading platform.

news.headline: DeFi dominance declines as bitcoin, XRP steal crypto-market spotlight

news.summary:
- 1inch regained dominance in the DEX aggregator space, reaching 60% market share in May, up from 32% in March.  \
- The surge follows its strategic expansion to Solana in late April, aimed at capturing memecoin trading volume.  \
- Total DEX aggregator trade volume hit a record $52 billion in May, with 1inch accounting for $31.5 billion.  \
- The move has squeezed competitors like CoW Swap, Kyber, Paraswap, and Curve, prompting others to accelerate multi-chain strategies.  \
- Solana continues to lead in new token launches, with 86% of new tokens deployed there versus 10% on Base.  \
- The rapid market share shift underscores the fluid, competitive nature of DEX aggregation driven by ecosystem expansion and product development.

news.headline: Biggest Crypto Exploits and Hacks of 2023
news.summary:
- Crypto hacks and exploits continued to occur in 2023, but it was not as bad as the previous year.\
- TRM Labs reported that $1.7 billion was lost to hacks in 2023, a significant decrease from the $4 billion stolen in 2022.\
- Some of the biggest hacks of the year include Mixin ($200 million), Euler Finance ($197 million), Poloniex ($126 million), Atomic wallet ($100 million), Curve ($60 million), Kyber ($48 million), and Stake ($40 million).\
- Many of the hacked platforms were able to recover a portion of the stolen funds or freeze the addresses associated with the hackers.\
- The FBI attributed the Stake hack to the North Korea-linked hacker organization Lazarus Group.

news.headline: Eternum Season 1: Starknet's Biggest MMO Expands Its Battlefield
news.summary:
- Eternum, built on Starknet’s Dojo engine, launched Season 0 in December 2024, attracting hundreds of players experimenting with alliance-building and territory control.  \
- Season 1 introduces AI-powered NPC agents called Daydreams, each with unique traits, behaviors, and goals, adding unpredictability to gameplay.  \
- New onboarding options include Village Lords using stablecoins for quick entry and Realm Lords minting or buying Realms NFTs for full control and conquest capabilities.  \
- Core gameplay updates feature real-time, stamina-based combat, terrain effects, troop tiering, and a simplified Labor mechanic to ease resource management.  \
- The season is divided into phases: the Season Pass (starting April 30), Settling (May 7), and Empire (May 14), with ongoing resource trading, alliances, and battles.  \
- Players can pursue various strategies—territory conquest, resource economics, or agent hunting—with significant token pools ($LORDS and $STRK) incentivizing participation.

- **Compensation Plan Details**: The plan includes distributing 27 million Safu Recovery Tokens (SRT) to affected users, with 4% of MGP token supply allocated for repayment.\
